CONSENT.OPEN_PLAYER_APP_HINT: Review and update your boundaries and play preferences.
CONSENT.OPEN_GM_APP_HINT: Review player forms and the table summary.
CONSENT.PLAYER_INTRO: Choose what can be narrated freely, what needs a conversation, and what must stay out of the game. Every change is saved automatically.
CONSENT.PROGRESS_REASSURANCE: You do not need to answer everything: this is neither a questionnaire nor a personality quiz. Only share what feels useful.
CONSENT.TRANSFER_HINT: Export your answers to use them in another world or at another table.
CONSENT.IMPORT_CONFIRM_WARNING: Importing will permanently replace every answer and note currently saved in this world. Export your current form first if you want to keep a copy.
CONSENT.IMPORT_CONFIRM_SUMMARY: Form exported by {playerName}: {answered} answers provided across {total} topics.
CONSENT.IMPORT_ERROR: This file is not a valid consent form.
CONSENT.GM_DASHBOARD_INTRO: Spot shared boundaries, prepare necessary conversations, and review each form without leaving the table.
CONSENT.NOTES_HELP: Add any context or nuance that may help the GM.
CONSENT.PRIVACY_NOTICE: Private form protected by Foundry permissions: you and this world's GMs.
CONSENT.SAVE_ERROR: Save failed — your latest changes may not be stored.
CONSENT.STORAGE_ERROR: Private form storage could not be initialized. Check the Foundry console before continuing.
CONSENT.STORAGE_DELETE_BLOCKED: This journal contains Consent Form technical data and cannot be deleted.
CONSENT.MIGRATION_SUCCESS: Your former actor form was imported into your new consent form.
CONSENT.CONTENT_WARNING: This form lists potentially difficult subjects. You may leave any category closed or unanswered and return later.
CONSENT.FORBIDDEN_CHART_HINT: Number of hard limits by category.
CONSENT.DISCUSS_CHART_HINT: Number of points to clarify by category.
CONSENT.NO_PLAYERS: There are no players in this world.
CONSENT.TABLE_SUMMARY_HINT: Hard limits appear first, followed by topics that require a conversation.
CONSENT.NO_PRIORITY: No boundary or discussion point has been reported yet.
CONSENT.NO_PLAYER_BOUNDARIES: This player has not identified any topic to discuss or forbid.
CONSENT.GM_NOTE_HINT: Record nuances discussed with this player, agreed veils, or precautions to keep in mind.
CONSENT.GM_NOTE_PLACEHOLDER: For example: keep this off-screen, stop the description before…
CONSENT.X_CARD_MESSAGE: The scene stops here. Nobody needs to explain why. Take a moment, then resume in a different direction.
CONSENT.X_CARD_GM_NOTIFICATION: An X-Card was triggered: stop the scene.
CONSENT.X_CARD_CATEGORY_PROMPT: If you wish, quickly identify the relevant category. Only your amber or red categories are shown.
CONSENT.X_CARD_DETAIL_PRIVACY: Your identity and precise choice are sent only to GMs. Other players see only the anonymous X-Card.
CONSENT.X_CARD_TOPIC_PROMPT: Which topic required the scene to stop?
CONSENT.X_CARD_NO_FLAGGED_TOPIC: Your form contains no amber or red topic. You may continue without giving details.
CONSENT.X_CARD_NO_DETAIL: The person chose not to specify the relevant topic.
CONSENT.X_CARD_COOLDOWN: The X-Card was already sent. You can trigger it again in {seconds}s.
CONSENT.X_CARD_NO_GM: No GM is currently connected. The stop remains visible on your screen, but it cannot be relayed to the table.
CONSENT.X_CARD_SEND_ERROR: The X-Card could not be relayed to the table. Notify the other people directly.
CONSENT.TITLE: Consent Form
CONSENT.LEVELS_TITLE: Each theme can be classified according to a three-level system:
CONSENT.LEVEL_NARRATIVE_FULL: Free narration: The theme can be addressed without restriction.
CONSENT.LEVEL_DISCUSS_FULL: Subject to discussion: The theme can be addressed, but only after a discussion to clarify the limits.
CONSENT.LEVEL_FORBIDDEN_FULL: Forbidden theme: The theme is completely excluded from the game.
CONSENT.ALERT_GM: {playerName} wants to bring something to your attention.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_ACROPHOBIA: Intense fear of heights or open spaces, often associated with the fear of falling.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_AEROPHOBIA: Irrational fear of flying or being in an airplane, often related to the fear of air accidents.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_AGORAPHOBIA: Fear of open spaces, public places, or crowds, often accompanied by the fear of being unable to escape.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_ARACHNOPHOBIA: Fear of spiders, sometimes extended to other arthropods like scorpions.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_BELONEPHOBIA: Excessive fear of needles or sharp objects, often related to medical procedures.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_CLAUSTROPHOBIA: Fear of enclosed or confined spaces, with the fear of running out of air or being unable to escape.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_COULROPHOBIA: Fear of clowns, often due to their exaggerated and unpredictable appearance.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_CYNOPHOBIA: Fear of dogs, sometimes related to a traumatic experience or the fear of being bitten.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_ENTOMOPHOBIA: Fear of insects, often exacerbated by their appearance or unpredictable behavior.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_HEMATOPHOBIA: Fear of blood, which can cause fainting at the sight of blood or during an injury.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_HERPETOPHOBIA: Fear of reptiles or amphibians, such as snakes and lizards.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_LOSSOFCONTROLPHOBIA: Fear of losing control of oneself or one's actions, which can lead to unpredictable or dangerous situations.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_NECROPHOBIA: Fear of corpses or anything related to death and decomposition.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_NYCTOPHOBIA: Fear of the dark or darkness, often related to the fear of the unknown or invisible dangers.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_OPHIOPHOBIA: Intense fear of snakes, often associated with an aversion to their appearance or behavior.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_PHASMOPHOBIA: Fear of ghosts or spirits, generally related to supernatural beliefs.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_TERATOPHOBIA: Fear of monsters or deformed creatures, often stemming from stories or legends.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_THANATOPHOBIA: Fear of death or the end of life, sometimes related to the anxiety of the unknown or loss of control.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_CURRENTWARS: Reference to ongoing armed conflicts around the world, with political and humanitarian implications.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_HISTORICALWARS: Reference to armed conflicts of the past, with consequences for populations and territories.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_CURRENTGENOCIDES: Reference to massacres and persecutions of ethnic or religious groups, with issues of justice and remembrance.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_HISTORICALGENOCIDES: Reference to the mass exterminations of the past, with their repercussions on societies and cultures.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_TERRORISTATTACKS: Reference to acts of terrorism, with civilian victims and political consequences.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_INDUSTRIALDISASTERS: Reference to industrial disasters, such as explosions, toxic leaks or collapses.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_NATURALDISASTERS: Reference to devastating natural events, such as earthquakes, tsunamis or hurricanes.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_PANDEMICS: Reference to global epidemics, such as infectious diseases or deadly viruses.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_MIGRATIONCRISIS: Reference to massive population movements, with humanitarian and political issues.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_FAMINESANDPOVERTY: Reference to food crises and precarious living conditions, with consequences for health and survival.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_POLITICALEVENTS: Reference to major political events, such as elections, revolutions, or coups.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_PARODIESANDSATIRES: Reference to humorous or critical works, with caricatures or distortions of reality.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_CORPORALPUNISHMENT: Reference to physical sanctions inflicted on individuals, often in a disciplinary or punitive context.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_PHYSICALTORTURE: Reference to abuse inflicted on people to obtain information or to punish them, with traumatic consequences.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_EXECUTIONS: Reference to the killing of condemned people, often in a judicial or political context.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_EXILE: Reference to the expulsion of an individual from their territory or community, with risks to their safety and survival.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_PUBLICHUMILIATION: Reference to the degradation of a person's image or reputation in public, with psychological consequences.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_SOLITARYCONFINEMENT: Reference to the confinement of an individual alone in a cell, with detrimental effects on their mental health.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_MENTALMANIPULATION: Reference to the psychological control exerted over a person to subdue or condition them, with consequences on their identity.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_PSYCHOLOGICALTORTURE: Reference to mental abuse inflicted on individuals to break or control them, with psychological trauma.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_INCARCERATION: Reference to detention in prison or a detention center, with difficult living conditions and health risks.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_FORCEDLABOR: Reference to the exploitation of an individual's labor against their will, often in inhumane conditions.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_RESTRICTIONOFFREEDOM: Reference to the limitation of a person's rights and movements, with constraints on their daily life.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_RELIGIOUSPUNISHMENTS: Reference to sanctions imposed by a religious authority, often related to prohibitions or dogmas.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_CULTURALPUNISHMENTS: Reference to social or traditional sanctions, often related to specific norms or values.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_IMPLICITROMANCE: Reference to suggested or implied romantic relationships, without details.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_EXPLICITROMANCE: Reference to detailed or explicit romantic relationships, with intimate scenes.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_ROMANCEBETWEENPCS: Reference to romantic relationships between player characters, with emotional implications.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_ROMANCEBETWEENNPCS: Reference to romantic relationships between non-player characters, with social interactions.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_ROMANCEPCNPC: Reference to romantic relationships between player characters and non-player characters, with narrative stakes.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_POLYAMORY: Reference to non-monogamous romantic relationships, with multiple consenting partners.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_INFIDELITY: Reference to betrayal or cheating in a romantic relationship, with emotional consequences.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_FORBIDDENRELATIONSHIPS: Reference to romantic relationships prohibited by social norms or family constraints.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_UNCONSCIOUSLOVE: Reference to unshared or unconfessed love, with hidden or repressed feelings.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_TOXICRELATIONSHIPS: Reference to unhealthy or destructive romantic relationships, with abusive or manipulative behaviors.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_UNREQUITEDLOVE: Reference to unreciprocated or unreturned love, with disappointed expectations or emotional suffering.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_FORCEDMARRIAGE: Reference to a union imposed on individuals without their consent, often for political or family reasons.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_SEXIMPLICIT: Reference to sexual relations suggested or implied, without details.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_SEXEXPLICIT: Reference to detailed or explicit sexual relations, with intimate scenes.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_SEXBETWEENPCS: Reference to sexual relations between player-characters, with emotional implications, narrative and ethical issues such as consent.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_SEXBETWEENNPCS: Reference to sexual relations between non-player characters, with social and political interactions.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_SEXPCNPC: Reference to sexual relations between player and non-player characters, with narrative and ethical issues such as consent.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_SOFTBDSM: Reference to light BDSM practices, often centered on consensual power plays, without intense physical violence.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_MODERATEBDSM: Reference to intermediate-level BDSM practices, involving more marked domination and submission, with moderate physical and psychological aspects.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_EXTREMEBDSM: Reference to advanced, often more intense BDSM practices that include elements of physical pain, advanced psychological domination and scenarios involving severe humiliation or extreme power plays.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_SEXWORK: Reference to prostitution or other forms of sex work, with social, economic and ethical issues such as stigmatization, exploitation and consent.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_SEXUALVIOLENCE: Reference to sexual assault, rape and abuse, with traumatic consequences.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_SEXUALMANIPULATION: Reference to sexual coercion, abuse of power or emotional manipulation in a sexual context, with psychic consequences.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_INCEST: Reference to sexual relationships between family members, often associated with abuse of power and trauma.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_PAEDOPHILIA: Reference to sexual attraction to children, with sexual abuse and psychological trauma.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_ZOOPHILIA: Reference to sexual attraction to animals, with animal abuse and violence.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_NECROPHILIA: Reference to sexual attraction to corpses, with acts of desecration and serious crimes.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_ABANDONMENT: Reference to the situation of a person left alone or without support, with emotional and psychological consequences.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_DOMESTICVIOLENCE: Reference to physical, psychological or sexual violence in the home, with abuse of power and trauma.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_MURDERSANDASSASSINATIONS: Reference to deliberate or premeditated homicide, with criminal and moral consequences.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_PHYSICALAGGRESSION: Reference to attacks or physical violence, with injuries and after-effects.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_BULLYING: Reference to bullying, intimidation or persecution, with abuse of power and psychological trauma.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_MUTILATIONSANDAMPUTATIONS: Reference to serious injury, mutilation or removal of limbs, with disabilities and physical trauma.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_CHILDABUSE: Reference to the physical or psychological abuse of children, including violence and trauma.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_ANIMALABUSE: Reference to animal abuse, with acts of cruelty and suffering.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_REALISTICCOMBAT: Reference to violent, bloody clashes, with detailed descriptions of injuries and deaths.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_VERBALABUSE: Reference to insults, threats or verbal humiliation, with attacks on self-esteem and dignity.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_INSTITUTIONALVIOLENCE: Reference to abuse of power and discrimination in institutions, with injustice and oppression.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_RELIGIOUSPERSECUTION: Reference to discrimination and violence against religious beliefs or practices, with conflict and persecution.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_ETHNICPERSECUTION: Reference to discrimination and violence against ethnic groups or minorities, with conflict and persecution.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_MEDICALEXPERIMENTATION: Reference to abusive or unethical medical practices, with human rights violations and health risks.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_SLAVERY: Reference to forced servitude and the exploitation of human beings, with inhuman living conditions and violations of fundamental rights.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_DEMONICPOSSESSION: Reference to demonic possession or exorcisms, with religious beliefs and supernatural phenomena.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_RITUALSACRIFICES: Reference to human or animal offerings as part of religious or magical rituals, with barbaric practices and serious crimes.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_CANNIBALISM: Reference to the consumption of human flesh, with acts of barbarism and crimes against humanity.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_BODYTRANSFORMATIONS: Reference to physical alterations or genetic mutations, with metamorphoses and alterations of being.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_ABLEISM: Discrimination against people with disabilities, with negative prejudices and stereotypes.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_AFROPHOBIA: Discrimination against black people or people of African descent, with racial prejudices and discrimination.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_AGEISM: Discrimination against elderly or young people, with stereotypes and age-related discrimination.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_ANTISEMITISM: Discrimination against Jewish people, with religious or ethnic prejudices and discrimination.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_AROPHOBIA: Discrimination against aromantic people, with prejudices and discrimination related to romantic orientation.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_ASEXUALPHOBIA: Discrimination against asexual people, with prejudices and discrimination related to sexual orientation.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_BIPHOBIA: Discrimination against bisexual people, with prejudices and discrimination related to sexual orientation.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_CLASSISM: Discrimination against people from disadvantaged social classes, with socio-economic stereotypes and discrimination.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_COLORISM: Discrimination against people based on their skin color, with prejudices and discrimination based on pigmentation.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_FATPHOBIA: Discrimination against overweight or obese people, with prejudices and discrimination related to physical appearance.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_HETERONORMATIVITY: Representation of heterosexual relationships as the norm, with stereotypes and discrimination against sexual minorities.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_HETEROPHOBIA: Discrimination against heterosexual people, with prejudices and discrimination related to sexual orientation.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_HOMOPHOBIA: Discrimination against homosexual people, with prejudices and discrimination related to sexual orientation.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_ISLAMOPHOBIA: Discrimination against Muslim people, with religious or cultural prejudices and discrimination.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_INTERSEXPHOBIA: Discrimination against intersex people, with prejudices and discrimination related to diversity.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_LANGUAGEDISCRIMINATION: Discrimination against people based on their language or accent, with cultural prejudices and discrimination.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_LESBOPHOBIA: Discrimination against homosexual women, with prejudices and discrimination related to sexual orientation.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_NEURODIVERGENCEDISCRIMINATION: Discrimination against neurodivergent people, with prejudices and discrimination related to brain function.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_NONBINARYPHOBIA: Discrimination against non-binary people, with prejudices and discrimination related to gender identity.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_PANPHOBIA: Discrimination against pansexual people, with prejudices and discrimination related to sexual orientation.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_RACISM: Discrimination against people based on their ethnic origin or skin color, with racial prejudices and discrimination.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_RELIGIOUSDISCRIMINATION: Discrimination against people based on their belief or religious practice, with religious prejudices and discrimination.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_ROMAPHOBIA: Discrimination against Romani or Gypsy people, with ethnic prejudices and discrimination.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_SEXISM: Discrimination against people based on their sex or gender, with sexist prejudices and discrimination.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_SYSTEMICRACISM: Discrimination against people based on their ethnic origin or skin color within a social or institutional system, with structural inequalities and injustices.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_SIZEISM: Discrimination against people based on their size, with prejudices and discrimination related to physical appearance.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_THINPHOBIA: Discrimination against thin or skinny people, with prejudices and discrimination related to physical appearance.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_TRANSPHOBIA: Discrimination against transgender people, with prejudices and discrimination related to gender identity.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_XENOPHOBIA: Discrimination against foreign or foreign-born people, with prejudices and discrimination related to nationality or culture.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_CANCER: Reference to a disease characterized by the uncontrolled proliferation of abnormal cells, with heavy treatments and risks of recurrence.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_NEURODEGENERATIVEDISEASES: Reference to nervous system disorders, such as Alzheimer's disease, Parkinson's disease, or multiple sclerosis, with progressive and disabling symptoms.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_SIDAVIH: Reference to infection with the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) and its advanced stage, acquired immunodeficiency syndrome (AIDS), which affects the immune system and can lead to serious, often fatal complications.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_CHRONICDISEASES: Reference to long-term conditions, such as diabetes, hypertension, or asthma, with regular treatments and possible complications.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_DEPRESSION: Reference to a mood disorder characterized by deep sadness, loss of interest and pleasure, sleep and appetite disturbances.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_SUICIDE: Reference to the voluntary act of ending one's own life, as well as associated themes such as suicidal thoughts, attempts, or the emotional and social consequences for loved ones.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_PSYCHOSES: Reference to a group of mental disorders characterized by a loss of contact with reality, including delusions, hallucinations, and thought disorders.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_ANXIETYDISORDERS: Reference to a group of psychological disorders characterized by excessive worry, irrational fears, and an increased response to stress, often disproportionate to the actual situation.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_EATINGDISORDERS: Reference to disturbed or irregular eating behaviors, often related to a distorted body perception or psychological issues.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_SELFHARM: Reference to self-destructive behaviors, such as cutting, scratching, or burning, often related to emotional or psychological disorders.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_MISCARRIAGE: Reference to the spontaneous termination of pregnancy before the 20th week, with emotional and physical consequences for the mother and the couple.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_INFERTILITY: Reference to the inability to conceive a child naturally, with emotional, social, and medical consequences.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_ABORTION: Reference to the voluntary termination of pregnancy, with ethical, legal, and medical issues.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_TOTALITARIANIDEOLOGIES: Reference to authoritarian political systems that seek to control all aspects of social, economic, and political life, with human rights violations and repressions.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_AUTHORITARIANCOMMUNISM: Reference to communist regimes that have exercised authoritarian control over society, economy, and politics, with human rights violations and political repressions.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_THEOCRATICREGIMES: Reference to political systems based on religious principles, with a fusion between political and religious power, often at the expense of individual freedoms.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_CHAUVINISM: Reference to excessive and aggressive nationalism, with glorification of the nation and hostility towards other countries or ethnic groups.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_INDOCTRINATION: Reference to the teaching of beliefs or ideologies without critical thinking, with mental manipulation and submission to authority.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_RELIGIOUSFANATICISM: Reference to extreme and intolerant religious belief, with rigid adherence to religious dogma or practice, often to the detriment of human rights and individual freedoms.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_SECTARIANGROUPS: Reference to extremist religious or ideological movements, with sectarian practices, mental manipulation and psychological abuse.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_PROSELYTIZING: Reference to the aggressive promotion of a belief or ideology, with attempts at forced conversion or emotional manipulation.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_RACIALSUPREMACISM: Reference to the superiority of one ethnic or cultural group over others, often with the aim of justifying social, economic, political and cultural inequalities.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_EUGENICIDEOLOGIES: Reference to the idea that genetic improvement of the human population can be achieved through selection practices, favoring certain individuals deemed “superior” and excluding those considered “inferior”, often to the detriment of human rights.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_HATEGROUPS: Reference to organizations or movements that promote hatred, violence or discrimination against ethnic, religious, cultural or social groups, with extremist rhetoric and acts of violence.
CONSENT.DEFINITION_COMPLOTISM: Reference to the belief in unfounded conspiracy theories, often based on misconceptions or prejudice, with consequences for the perception of reality and risks for democracy and social cohesion in a society.
